original_hypothesis.title: Storage.updateHookPath can create a silent guard bypass if hook is enabled but does not enforce baseline selector blocks
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Guard.checkTransaction forwards to hook.onTransaction(safe,to,value,data) when a hook is configured for a target and hookOnTransaction is enabled, and then skips _checkTransaction entirely. GUARD_ADMIN can map any target contract to any hook via Guard.updateHookPath(), and enable onTransaction via updateHookStatus(). If a hook implementation is permissive/buggy, safe owners can execute previously blocked operations (e.g., setGuard, enableModule, NFT transfers) by targeting that contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a realistic operational failure mode during hook rollout/upgrade: a single incorrect hook can undermine the guard. While it is admin-mediated (low realism), it is practical and high impact operationally.
deployment_assumptions: GUARD_ADMIN may configure hooks for production targets.
Hook contracts are not formally verified to enforce the baseline guard rules.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Guard.sol checkTransaction() routes to hook and does not call _checkTransaction when hook active.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Hook path bypasses base selector checks entirely

preconditions: A hook is set for a target contract and hookOnTransaction is enabled.
Hook implementation allows (does not revert) for restricted operations.
Safe owner can call the target contract with restricted selector.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Security logic is delegated to external hooks without minimum enforcement
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time after hook is enabled

operational_questions: Which targets have hooks configured in production?
Do hooks implement a superset of _checkTransaction restrictions?
Is there monitoring/kill-switch to disable a misbehaving hook quickly?
validation_requirements: Review each hook's onTransaction logic against blocked selector set
Property test: for any rented asset, transfer/approve selectors must revert even with hooks

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Find a target contract with an active hook
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: From rental safe, call restricted NFT transfer/approval/module-change function against that target
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Hook permits it, guard does not perform selector checks, operation succeeds

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Theft of rented NFTs; disabling stop module or guard possible

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: STORE.updateHookPath/updateHookStatus changes; unusual safe transactions routed via hook targets
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Rented assets transferred out despite active rental
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Calls to setGuard/enableModule succeeding during active rentals

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
